overview: The Village of Weston Board of Trustees approved increased park and recreation impact fees, passed several ordinances including amendments to mobile food vendor licensing and e-bike regulations, and held extensive discussion about farmers market parking issues at Kennedy Park. The board also adjourned the Board of Review to June 15, 2026.

agenda.item: Board of Review called to order and adjourned to June 15, 2026
Board of Trustees called to order, Pledge of Allegiance, Roll Call
Public hearing on park and recreation impact fees (Ordinance 26-010 and Resolution 2026-015)
Acknowledge reports from boards, committees, and commissions with chair updates
Department reports (Administrative, Clerk, Finance, Police, Parks, Planning, Public Works, Technology)
Consent agenda including vouchers and license renewals
Ordinances including mobile food vendor, standing committees, nuisances, and e-bikes
Discussion on farmers market location at Kennedy Park

discussions.item: Park and Recreation Impact Fee Amendment
discussions.body: Staff presented proposed fee increases from $300 per single family unit to $761, from $250 to $563 for duplex units, and $250 to $495 for multifamily units, based on the 2022 public facilities needs assessment. Trustee Barb supported the fees as justified given population increases. Jim Pitel during public comment criticized the lack of detailed data on actual park maintenance costs. Ordinance 26-010 was approved with motion by Lope Sorrell, second by Zagami, passed unanimously. Resolution 2026-015 was approved with motion by Hermling, second by Clark, passed unanimously.

The board debated amendments to section 1.08 of the officials handbook. Discussion centered on removing specific health/safety/welfare justification requirements to make the policy more broad. Trustees agreed chairs should not have to evaluate excuses. Beck moved to amend by striking sentences requiring health/safety reasons, seconded by Clark. The amendment passed with two no votes from Hermling and Lope Sorrell. The original motion as amended also passed with two no votes from the same trustees.

Chief Finkey explained the ordinance creates a reciprocal inspection agreement between Safer, Marshfield, Wisconsin Rapids, Stevens Point, Clover, and Riverside. Under this system, a fire inspection sticker from any participating department is recognized by all others, eliminating redundant inspections for food trucks operating in multiple communities. Motion by Lope Sorrell, approved unanimously.

discussions.item: Secondhand Dealer License for Scoffield Coin and Hobby LLC
discussions.body: Discussion addressed why secondhand dealer licenses are required - primarily for tracking potentially stolen merchandise through a police-accessible database and requiring a 30-day hold period. Since the business at 5703 Jamar Street hasn't received occupancy yet, the board amended the motion to make approval contingent on satisfactory background check completion and receipt of occupancy permit. Approved unanimously as amended.

discussions.item: Farmers Market Location at Kennedy Park
discussions.body: Extensive discussion addressed parking concerns at the new Kennedy Park location after the previous Camp Phillips/Scoffield site was sold for development. Trustee Beck raised safety concerns about cars parking on grass along Alderson. The market has grown from 25 stalls to 53 vendors who can now keep vehicles with them. Renee explained the market is not losing money and receives grants for SNAP/EBT token programs. No formal action taken; discussion only as this was an evolving situation to monitor.

Public Works Director Michael reported on efforts to resolve the speed limit issue raised at previous meetings. The DOT engineering study indicated 35 mph, but traffic data showed 85th percentile speed at 45 mph. Construction delayed completing a second speed study. A resident noted the current speed is inconsistent with the village ordinance. Staff indicated this would need to come back to the board, possibly at a joint meeting.

publicComment: Jim Pitel of 52nd Street offered public comment on multiple topics. He criticized spending $108,000 on outside engineering consultants (agenda items 68-69) when an engineering technician position remains unfilled, questioning why the administrator hasn't filled approved positions. He objected that Lisa Beck was introduced as a Safer board member at the April Fire Department meeting before being officially appointed. He criticized the lack of information on Kennedy Park in the meeting packet despite trustees requesting a detailed report. During the impact fee hearing, Pitel argued the village lacks detailed data on actual park maintenance costs to justify the fee amounts.
actionItems: Park and recreation impact fees increased to $761 single family, $563 duplex, $495 multifamily effective with ordinance passage
Board of Review adjourned to June 15, 2026
Golden Mua and Stephanie Daniels appointed to CLIPS committee
Comprehensive Plan steering committee meeting scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 6:00 PM as joint meeting with Plan Commission
Staff to prepare intergovernmental service agreement for building inspection assistance to Town of Ringle for tornado-damaged homes
Remote meeting attendance policy amended to remove health/safety justification requirements
Speed limit study for Weston Avenue to be completed after construction ends and brought back to board
Committee/commission applications to be included in board packets going forward
Electronics drive date corrected to June 6th
Secondhand dealer license for Scoffield Coin and Hobby LLC approved pending occupancy and background check
title: Board of Trustees
